newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. The headline SEK ~2bn US AI data-center cooling order was announced April 24 and books into Q2 intake; it pre-dates the prior cases and is already in the thesis. The -14% move since June 9 is price/flow, not a new fundamental event.

sources.label: Munters wins SEK 2.0bn modular AI cooling order (Apr 24)
Munters lands SEK 2bn US AI data center cooling order (TipRanks)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent thermal/water node: data-center cooling angle is now visible, not latent. Q1 order intake +32% and the SEK 2bn AI order are known and in the mark; no new news since June 9. Margin slippage caps upside, so hold rank 36.
verdicts.bull: Surging DCT order intake (+32% Q1), SEK 2bn AI modular-cooling win and a CDU/CRAH platform position Munters as a scaled liquid-cooling enabler.
verdicts.neutral: Demand is exceptional but earnings/margins weakened in Q1; data-center exposure now well covered.
verdicts.bear: Cooling angle is no longer under-followed; margin pressure and rich expectations limit residual alpha.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 order intake booking the SEK 2bn AI order; liquid-cooling capacity scale-up.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Margin erosion as mix shifts; execution on rapid DCT capacity expansion.

verdicts.rankRationale: Master bottleneck list: already rank 1, so no upside move possible. Cooling/air-treatment bottleneck thesis fully intact with the SEK 2bn AI order; a -14% flow-driven reset improves risk/reward and supports holding the top slot.
verdicts.bull: Scarce, scaled exposure to the data-center cooling bottleneck; record AI orders and a deep negative-YTD reset improve entry risk/reward at the top slot.
verdicts.neutral: Best-positioned cooling name on the list, but Q1 margin softness tempers the top-rank conviction.
verdicts.bear: Top-ranked into margin weakness; any DCT order or pricing wobble would challenge the #1 slot.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued record data-center cooling order intake and liquid-cooling scale.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Margin slippage or order lumpiness undermining the bottleneck premium.
